**PROJECT FULLY GENERATED BY LLM. DO NOT USE IT IF LLM GENERATED CODE IS NOT ALLOWED IN YOUR PROJECT**

# ink-to-odin

An [Odin](https://odin-lang.org/) port of [inkle's ink](https://github.com/inkle/ink) narrative scripting runtime — the engine behind *80 Days*, *Heaven's Vault*, *Sorcery!*, and many other story-driven games.

This is the **runtime player only**. Authoring still happens in the original `.ink` language, compiled to JSON with inklecate. This package consumes that JSON and runs the story.

Conformance: byte-for-byte parity with inkle's reference C# runtime across all 10 seeded random walks of *The Intercept*.

## Quick start

You need [Odin](https://odin-lang.org/docs/install/) and a git client. Tests against the reference runtime additionally need [.NET 6+](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download).

```bash
git clone --recursive https://github.com/Wc4ever/ink-to-odin.git
cd ink-to-odin

# Play TheIntercept in the terminal:
odin run ./examples/intercept_cli
```

You should see "They are keeping me waiting." followed by a numbered choice prompt. Type a number, press enter; `q` quits.

All shell snippets in this README are run from the repo root unless noted.

## Using the runtime in your game

The package lives at `ink/` and is self-contained — no Odin dependencies outside `core`. Two ways to wire it into your project:

- Drop the `ink/` directory next to your sources and `import ink "./ink"` (or whatever relative path).
- Or put the repo on Odin's collection path: `odin run ./mygame -collection:ink=path/to/ink-to-odin/ink`, then `import "ink"`.

Minimal embedding:

```odin
package mygame

import "core:fmt"
import "core:os"
import ink "ink"

main :: proc() {
    json_bytes, ok := os.read_entire_file("my_story.ink.json")
    if !ok {
        fmt.eprintln("could not read story")
        return
    }
    defer delete(json_bytes)

    story: ink.Compiled_Story
    if err := ink.compiled_story_load(&story, string(json_bytes)); err != .None {
        fmt.eprintfln("load: %v", err)
        return
    }
    defer ink.compiled_story_destroy(&story)

    state: ink.Story_State
    ink.story_state_init(&state, &story)
    defer ink.story_state_destroy(&state)

    for {
        // Drain text up to the next choice or the end of the story.
        for ink.story_state_can_continue(&state) {
            if !ink.story_continue(&state) do break

            text := ink.story_current_text(&state)
            defer delete(text)
            // ... render `text` to your UI ...
        }

        choices := ink.story_current_choices(&state)
        if len(choices) == 0 do break

        // Present choices[i].text to the player, get their selection (0..len-1):
        picked_index := 0  // replace with the actual pick
        ink.story_choose_choice_index(&state, picked_index)
    }
}
```

Reading globals is polling-only — no observer/callback API. After each `story_continue` call, query `ink.variable_state_get_global(&state.variables_state, "name")` for any value you care about.

Backend-agnostic by design: nothing in `ink/` depends on a renderer or game framework. The `examples/intercept_cli/` consumer is a terminal app; a raylib (or anything else) consumer is just a different driver around the same `Story_State`.

## What's supported

Verified against TheIntercept (all 10 seeds, byte-identical to upstream):

- Knots, stitches, gathers, weaves, nested choices
- Diverts (absolute & relative paths), function calls, tunnels, tunnel-onwards
- Variables (`VAR`, `temp`, ref params), assignments, variable pointers
- Conditionals (`{cond: A | B}`), once-only choices
- Sequences and shuffles (deterministic, RNG-matched to upstream)
- Glue, function-bracket whitespace trimming, output stream snapshot/rewind
- Native operators (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `!`, `&&`, `||`, `MIN`, `MAX`, `POW`, unary negate, `FLOOR`, `CEILING`, `INT`, `FLOAT`)
- Visit counts, turn indices, `READ_COUNT`, `TURNS_SINCE`
- Tags (legacy `#tag` and modern `Begin_Tag`/`End_Tag`)
- Choice-thread forking, full state-snapshot rollback at newline lookahead

## What's not supported (yet)

These don't appear in TheIntercept and aren't exercised by the conformance tests, so they're effectively unverified or unimplemented:

- `LIST` types — declarations and operations are in the loader, but list ops aren't fully wired in the evaluator
- Multi-flow (`SwitchFlow`, parallel story branches)
- External function bindings — diverts marked `x()` error out
- Save/load round-trip — `state_to_json` exists; the loader (`state_load_json`) does not yet
- Story threading (`thread`, `<- thread`) — the call-stack layer supports threads, the evaluator's `Start_Thread` command is stubbed
- `RANDOM(min, max)` and `SEED_RANDOM` — control commands stubbed (the deterministic sequence shuffle uses the same algorithm but doesn't expose the user-facing RANDOM)
- Default invisible choices

If you have a story that hits one of these, file an issue with the `.ink` and a description of what should happen.

## How conformance is tested

`vendor/ink/` is a git submodule of [github.com/inkle/ink](https://github.com/inkle/ink) — the canonical upstream. Two test runners produce per-step logs in the same format:

- `tools/ink-test-runner/` (dotnet, references `vendor/ink/ink-engine-runtime/` directly): runs the *reference* C# runtime, writes `tests/golden/reference/seed_{0..9}.log`. Treated as source of truth.
- `tools/ink-test-runner-odin/` (Odin): runs *this* runtime, produces in-memory logs. Its `diff_test.odin` byte-diffs against the dotnet goldens.

Logs include public observables (`OUTPUT`, `TAGS`, `CHOICES`, `PICK`) and the full `state.ToJson()` snapshot per step (object keys recursively sorted, arrays in source order). Choice picks are seeded via a port of `System.Random` (`ink/net_random.odin`) so the same numerical RNG sequence drives both runtimes.

Regenerate goldens after a submodule bump:

```bash
# 1. Build inklecate from the submodule and recompile TheIntercept.ink
dotnet build vendor/ink/inklecate/inklecate.csproj -c Release
dotnet vendor/ink/inklecate/bin/Release/net6.0/inklecate.dll \
    tests/fixtures/the_intercept/TheIntercept.ink

# 2. Run the dotnet runner to produce reference logs
dotnet run --project tools/ink-test-runner/InkTestRunner.csproj
```

Run all tests:

```bash
odin test ./ink                          # unit tests, 53 of them
odin test ./tools/ink-test-runner-odin   # the conformance diff against reference logs
```

## Project layout

```
ink-to-odin/
├── ink/                           the package (import "path/to/ink")
│   ├── compiled_story.odin        Compiled_Story + lifecycle
│   ├── json_load.odin             parses inklecate's compiled JSON
│   ├── json_save.odin             state_to_json (round-trips with upstream)
│   ├── runtime_object.odin        Object + Object_Variant tagged union
│   ├── value.odin                 Int / Float / String / List / etc. value types
│   ├── path.odin                  Path navigation (knot.stitch.idx, with relative `^`)
│   ├── pointer.odin               (Container, index) execution position
│   ├── object.odin                root-walking, content-at-path, relative resolution
│   ├── call_stack.odin            frames, threads, temp variables
│   ├── variable_state.odin        globals, defaults, variable-pointer deref
│   ├── output_stream.odin         text accumulation, glue/trim, current_text/tags
│   ├── story_state.odin           the saveable runtime state (everything mutable)
│   ├── state_snapshot.odin        deep-copy/restore for newline lookahead
│   ├── story.odin                 the evaluator: story_continue, story_choose_choice_index
│   ├── net_random.odin            port of System.Random for seeded ops
│   └── *_test.odin                @(test) procs
│
├── examples/
│   ├── intercept_cli/             terminal player for TheIntercept
│   └── loader_smoke_test/         visual sanity check for the JSON loader
│
├── tests/
│   ├── fixtures/the_intercept/    vendored TheIntercept.ink + compiled .json
│   └── golden/reference/          dotnet runner output, source of truth
│
├── tools/
│   ├── ink-test-runner/           dotnet test runner (writes goldens)
│   └── ink-test-runner-odin/      Odin test runner + diff test
│
└── vendor/ink/                    git submodule of inkle's reference repo
```

## Acknowledgments & licensing

- [**inkle**](https://www.inklestudios.com/ink/) created the ink language and runtime. The reference runtime in `vendor/ink/` and *The Intercept* (`tests/fixtures/the_intercept/TheIntercept.ink`) are MIT-licensed, © 2016 inkle Ltd.
- The port is written in [Odin](https://odin-lang.org/).

The Odin port itself (everything outside `vendor/ink/` and `tests/fixtures/`) is MIT-licensed.

## Status

Alpha. The conformance bar is met (byte-equivalent on TheIntercept) but the runtime hasn't been exercised against many other stories. Expect to find bugs once you try yours — the diff test machinery makes them easy to localize.
